summary: Closed, unmerged tooling/docs PR that proposed adding `ninja format` and `ninja format-check` targets backed by a new Python wrapper around `git clang-format`, plus a contributing-doc update. The author reported local validation and a bot reported no GALE01 changes, but reviewer feedback questioned the implementation direction and suggested per-file Ninja formatting or pre-commit documentation instead.

key_files.role: New Python CLI wrapper for changed-line formatting through `git clang-format`, with `--check`, optional base revision, configurable clang-format binary, git-worktree detection, and missing-tool errors.
evidence_refs: pr_body: target uses a small Python wrapper around `git clang-format`

key_files.role: Adds generated Ninja rules/build targets named `format` and `format-check` that invoke `tools/format.py` and depend implicitly on the script and `.clang-format`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added clang_format and clang_format_check rules plus format/format-check builds

key_files.role: Updates auto-formatting instructions from direct `git clang-format` usage to `ninja format` and `ninja format-check` after project configuration.

what_changed.text: Added `tools/format.py`, a Python wrapper that runs `git clang-format --binary <clang-format>` from the repository root, optionally with a base revision.
evidence_refs: pr_body: `ninja format` runs the same changed-line workflow as `git clang-format`

what_changed.text: Implemented check mode by passing `--diff` to `git clang-format` and returning failure when the output contains a diff.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: tools/format.py appends `--diff` when check is true and checks for `diff --git`

what_changed.text: Added `format` and `format-check` Ninja targets in project generation.
evidence_refs: issue_comment-4359409090: author confirmed generated build.ninja has both targets

what_changed.text: Updated contributor documentation to recommend `ninja format` for formatting changed C/C++ lines and `ninja format-check` for checking without edits.

smart_moves.text: The wrapper explicitly checks for missing `clang-format` and `git-clang-format` and returns direct errors rather than relying on Python tracebacks.
The PR included targeted validation for syntax, project generation, target discovery, direct wrapper invocation, Ninja target execution, and diff cleanliness.
evidence_refs: issue_comment-4359427450: reran generated Ninja targets directly

smart_moves.text: The formatting workflow was scoped to changed lines instead of whole-repository formatting.
evidence_refs: pr_body: changed-line workflow and not reformatting the whole repository

decomp_lessons.lesson: For decomp tooling PRs, formatter/check targets should be evaluated not only for correctness but also for whether they exploit the build system's incremental model.
evidence_refs: issue_comment-4391744803: reviewer says #1543 intended per-file clang-format so ninja can track dirty files efficiently

decomp_lessons.lesson: A wrapper around existing tooling can preserve established changed-line behavior, but reviewer evidence in this PR indicates that may be insufficient if the requested feature was incremental per-file formatting.
evidence_refs: pr_body: wrapper around git clang-format keeps staged/diff-based behavior
issue_comment-4391744803: reviewer questions utility compared to per-file Ninja tracking

naming_conventions.text: The proposed public target names were `format` and `format-check`; internal Ninja rule names used `clang_format` and `clang_format_check`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: tools/project.py rule names and build outputs

naming_conventions.text: The Python wrapper used straightforward helper names matching their roles: `git_root`, `run_git_clang_format`, and `main`.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Questioned the utility of this implementation because issue #1543 was understood as per-file `clang-format` execution so Ninja can track dirty files efficiently.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Compared outsourcing the entire formatting operation to `git-clang-format` through a Python script unfavorably with using an existing commit hook workflow.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Suggested documenting pre-commit setup and adding it to `requirements.in` as a possible alternative.

follow_up_queries: PRs or issues discussing #1543 formatter target design
Ninja per-file clang-format dirty file tracking

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR state CLOSED, merged_at null, changed_files count 3
human_pr_text: PR body and issue comments loaded
text_corpus: bot report says GALE01 no changes
notes: The changed files and proposed behavior are well supported by the full diff and PR body.
Validation claims are author-reported and bot-reported in comments; intake did not rerun tools.
The PR was closed with `merged_at` null, so these changes should not be treated as accepted project standards or landed path facts.
There were no review comments and no formal review records in the slice; review feedback comes from an issue comment.
This PR is tooling/docs-focused and contains no assembly, matching, or decompiled C source tactics.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Formatting Ninja targets should match Ninja's incremental strengths
accepted_candidate_records.text: When proposing Ninja formatter targets, align the implementation with the intended incremental behavior, such as per-file tracking, rather than only wrapping an existing whole-operation formatter command.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider documenting pre-commit formatting setup instead of git-clang-format Ninja wrapper
source_update_candidates.text: Reviewer suggested that, given existing `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, the project may prefer documenting pre-commit setup and adding it to `requirements.in` over a Python wrapper around `git-clang-format`.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is reviewer feedback from a closed, unmerged PR and should be curated as a possible tooling/docs follow-up, not as an accepted standard.

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify desired implementation for issue #1543 format targets
source_update_candidates.text: Reviewer stated that the idea of #1543 was to run each file through `clang-format` individually so Ninja can track dirty files efficiently.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential design requirement for future tooling work; not accepted by this PR because the PR was closed unmerged.

rejection_notes: Do not record `ninja format` or `ninja format-check` as accepted project commands from this PR alone because `merged_at` is null and the final visible feedback objected to the implementation.
Do not treat author validation as proof that the tooling was adopted; it only supports that the submitted branch was locally exercised.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the explicit comment questioning utility and suggesting pre-commit documentation.
